title: Database Design (MongoDB)

questions.question_text: What is the main difference between SQL and NoSQL databases?
options: SQL is for web, NoSQL is for mobile
SQL is relational (structured, tables); NoSQL is non-relational (document, key-value)
questions.explanation: Relational databases use predefined schemas; NoSQL is schema-less/flexible.
MongoDB stores data in JSON-like documents.

questions.question_text: In relational databases, what is a 'Primary Key'?
options: A unique identifier for each record in a table
A field in one table that links to the Primary Key of another table (creating a relationship)
questions.explanation: This establishes relationships like 'User HAS MANY Posts'.
The four basic operations of persistent storage.

questions.question_text: In MongoDB, what is a 'Collection' equivalent to in SQL?
questions.explanation: Documents live inside collections; rows live inside tables.

options: Optimizing the structure to reduce data redundancy and improve integrity
questions.explanation: Normalization helps avoid data duplication across multiple tables.

questions.question_text: What is an 'Index' in a database?
options: A data structure that improves the speed of data retrieval (at the cost of slower writes)
questions.explanation: Indices are essential for performance on large datasets.

options: A set of properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) that guarantee database reliability
A fast way to delete data
questions.explanation: Transactions ensure that either all steps succeed or none do.

options: Object-Relational/Document Mapper; a tool to interact with the DB using your programming language objects instead of raw queries
questions.explanation: Mongoose (ODM) and Prisma (ORM) are popular choices.

options: A plan to hack the DB
The formal definition of the structure of the data (fields, types, constraints)
questions.explanation: Think of it as a 'blueprint' for your data.
A common example: One Author has many Books.

options: Purposefully adding redundant data to improve read performance (often used in NoSQL)
questions.explanation: It trades space for speed in specific use cases.

questions.question_text: Which property ensures a field must be filled in?
questions.explanation: Setting 'required: true' prevents saving incomplete data.

options: Moving the DB to a new city
Controlled version management of the database schema (updating tables/columns safely)
questions.explanation: Migrations track how your DB structure changes over time.
